source: chat: user-approved roadmap transition discussion after KB boundary refactor commit aa12f44
summary: Accepted roadmap transition decisions: keep Markdown links curated rather than exhaustive because graph owns machine relationships; roadmap hot set should contain active work only, with closed/cancelled history retained through git, builds, validation reports, and checkpoints.

accepted_decisions.summary: Markdown links remain useful for human navigation and intentional semantic dependencies, but they should be sparse curated links, not a manually maintained full graph.

The graph state machine owns exhaustive backlinks, drift detection, freshness, routing, and machine relationship discovery from frontmatter, links, builds, roadmap, validation, code/test facts, and source fingerprints.
Roadmap hot state should contain active work: todo, in_progress, blocked, verify, plus only short-lived closed/cancelled handoff entries.
Git is the full history mechanism for old roadmap tasks; builds and validation reports preserve semantic handoff/evidence when needed.

The current roadmap should transition by rewriting still-relevant active tasks, cancelling/defering obsolete active tasks, and creating follow-up work for active-roadmap retention if current tooling cannot safely prune old done tasks.

assumptions: Existing done tasks are not manually purged in this pass because canonical roadmap mutation should go through roadmap tooling or a dedicated retention migration.
The current transition may leave historical done tasks in roadmap storage until retention tooling is implemented.
open_questions: Exact retention window for closed/cancelled tasks is still policy/config work.
non_goals: Manually delete historical roadmap tasks without a validated retention mechanism.
Implement graph or roadmap retention code in this documentation transition pass.

roadmap: Rewrite active roadmap tasks to new KB refs and cancel/defer obsolete active work.
